title: fix(docs): Fix glossary FIXME tags
body:
<!--
Thanks for contributing a pull request! Please ensure you have taken a look at
the contribution guidelines: https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
-->

#### Reference Issues/PRs
Fixes #31628 

#### What does this implement/fix? Explain your changes.


#### Any other comments?
While working on [#31628](https://github.com/scikit-learn/scikit-learn/issues/31628), I encountered a `ModuleNotFoundError` due to a misnamed Sphinx extension (`sphinx-prompt` instead of `sphinx_prompt`). This PR also fixes that to ensure local doc builds (`make`) work correctly.

#### Setup
```
Python 3.13.3

   machine: macOS-15.5-arm64-arm-64bit-Mach-O

Python dependencies:
      sklearn: 1.8.dev0
          pip: 25.0.1
   setuptools: None
        numpy: 2.3.1
        scipy: 1.16.0
       Cython: 3.1.2
       pandas: 2.3.0
   matplotlib: 3.10.3
       joblib: 1.5.1
threadpoolctl: 3.6.0

Built with OpenMP: False
```

steps.operation: Fix Sphinx extension module name in the documentation build configuration by replacing the invalid entry `sphinx-prompt` with the correct importable module `sphinx_prompt` in the `extensions` list.
steps.reason: Sphinx loads extensions by importing the listed module names; using `sphinx-prompt` (with a hyphen) is not a valid Python module path and triggers `ModuleNotFoundError`, breaking local documentation builds (`make`).
steps.side_effects: Documentation builds will now successfully load the `sphinx_prompt` extension; if the dependency is missing in a given environment, the build will still fail but with the correct missing-module name. Any docs content relying on `sphinx-prompt` directives/roles will now resolve via the correct extension module.

step_plan.summary: Fixes local documentation builds by correcting the Sphinx extension name in `doc/conf.py` from `sphinx-prompt` to `sphinx_prompt`, preventing a `ModuleNotFoundError`.
